headline: GOLD HOLDS $4,850 INTO FOURTH STRAIGHT WEEKLY GAIN — Hormuz Ceasefire Lifts Metals Complex
snippet: Gold held near $4,850/oz Friday on track for its fourth consecutive weekly gain, after a US-Iran agreement to keep the Strait of Hormuz open triggered a sharp oil price drop that eased inflation fears across the precious and base metals complex.

headline: U.S. IMPORTS 95% OF ITS URANIUM — Nasdaq Newcomer Claims America's Largest Conventional Deposit
snippet: With U.S. uranium production covering only a fraction of domestic demand, a Nasdaq-listed junior is spotlighting what it calls the largest conventional uranium deposit in the country — coming as the spot U3O8 price hovers near $85/lb after briefly spiking above $100 in January.

headline: SILVER BREAKS ABOVE $80 — Up 143% Year-Over-Year as Sixth Straight Supply Deficit Looms
snippet: Silver surged past $80/oz this week, posting a 143% year-over-year gain as US-Iran diplomacy eased inflation fears and solar panel manufacturing continues to absorb available supply; the Silver Institute projects a sixth consecutive structural deficit of roughly 67 million ounces in 2026.

headline: OPINION: "WOULDN'T TOUCH USA RARE EARTH WITH A 10-FOOT POLE" — Motley Fool Skewers the $1.6B Government Deal
snippet: Despite a $1.6 billion deal with Washington, Motley Fool argues USA Rare Earth (USAR) grants the U.S. government equity without guaranteeing funding, leaving the company's mine and permanent magnet factory timeline dangerously uncertain — a cautionary read for retail investors buying the critical minerals narrative.

headline: SPROTT LAUNCHES World's First Ex-China Rare Earth ETF (REXC) on Nasdaq
snippet: Sprott listed the Rare Earths Ex-China ETF (Nasdaq: REXC) on April 15, offering pure-play exposure to rare earth miners operating entirely outside China as governments race to build domestic critical mineral supply chains independent of Beijing.

headline: HARVEST GOLD Maps 50 Drill Targets at Mosseau — 20-Hole 2026 Program Chases 105 g/t Au Discovery
snippet: Harvest Gold (TSX-V: HVG) defined 50 drill targets along the 32-kilometre Kiask River Mineralized Corridor in Quebec's Urban Barry Belt, with 20 priority holes planned for 2026 — eight within 500 metres of the discovery hole that assayed 105 g/t Au over 1.15 metres, with the system open in all directions.

headline: HYCROFT SHOWCASES 2,890 g/t Silver at Vortex — $189M Cash, Brimstone System Wide Open at Depth
snippet: At the Gold Forum Americas, Hycroft Mining (Nasdaq: HYMC) highlighted a Vortex drill hole returning 2,890 g/t silver and 33.70 g/t gold within a 53-metre interval, while the Brimstone silver system now extends at least 150 metres deeper than previously mapped — all funded by $189 million in unrestricted cash, zero debt.

headline: GOLD EYES $4,900 RESISTANCE — Asian Metals Roundup: Where the Bull Run Stalls and What Triggers the Next Leg
snippet: GoldSeek's April 14 Asian metals roundup flags gold approaching its 50-day moving average near $4,900 as key technical resistance, with new highs becoming probable above $5,250; silver is tracking a pattern closely resembling its 1972 consolidation before a historic multi-year breakout.

headline: LITHIUM PRICES DOUBLED IN Q1 — Battery Metal Stocks to Watch After China's April 1 Supply Reset
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate topped US$26,000/tonne in Q1 2026 as China's VAT export rebate cut on battery exports took effect April 1, a Zimbabwe production ban added supply pressure, and CATL's Jianxiawo mine remained suspended — setting up a volatile backdrop for junior lithium stocks in Q2.
